suggested_next_actions: Fix `/watchdog off` end-to-end so both hooks preserve the off override and the Stop hook short-circuits before analysis.
Add serialization around `watchdog-state.json` updates so concurrent Stop subprocesses cannot lose cooldown/dedup state.
Add a real HALT-positive integration test that stubs Codex, asserts exit code 2, and verifies the post-HALT cooldown/state mutation.

findings.title: `/watchdog off` is not actually honored
findings.explanation: The slash command docs promise that `off` writes an override and the hooks skip, but `session-task-class.py` drops an active `off` override on the floor: it only preserves non-`off` overrides and otherwise reclassifies the prompt, rewriting `.codex/task-class`. In `codex-watchdog.py`, `off` is also treated as an ordinary task class instead of a disabled state, so the Stop hook still runs under the default feature policy. The disable path therefore never works.
findings.recommendation: Preserve `off` as a terminal state in both hooks, and have the watchdog short-circuit before prefiltering when the active override is `off`.

findings.title: State updates are still racy across concurrent Stop hooks
findings.explanation: `watchdog-state.json` is loaded, mutated, and written back without any interprocess lock. Atomic `os.replace()` only prevents torn writes; it does not prevent lost updates when two Stop subprocesses run at the same time. On Windows, both processes can read the same state, both can decide to analyze, and the later write can overwrite the other process's `recent_wakes`, topic counts, or cooldown decrement. That breaks the dedup/cooldown behavior the spec calls out under subprocess races.
findings.recommendation: Add a file lock or a reload-and-merge retry loop around state transitions so only one Stop hook can commit a state change at a time.

findings.title: The suite still lacks a true-positive HALT path test
findings.explanation: `test_watchdog_fix.py` verifies verdict parsing and skip/downgrade behavior, but it never drives a concrete HALT-positive response through `main()`. There is no test that stubs `analyze_with_codex` to return a valid HALT verdict and then asserts exit code 2, stderr output, and the post-HALT state/cooldown changes. The highest-risk path in AC2 is therefore still unguarded.
findings.recommendation: Add an end-to-end HALT test that forces a real HALT verdict through `main()` and a follow-up stop that verifies the cooldown path.

verdict.rationale: The branch has a user-visible blocker: `/watchdog off` is effectively broken. It also leaves Stop-hook state updates unsafe under concurrent subprocesses, and the tests still do not cover a real HALT-positive path.
